title: Build Self-Healing AI Workflows with Claude Code
description: Learn to create agentic workflows that fix themselves when they break and adapt to changes automatically.

cards.title: Welcome to Agentic Automation
cards.body: You'll learn to build AI workflows that fix themselves when they break and adapt without manual debugging. We'll cover self-healing systems, natural language control, and building your first agentic workflow from scratch.

cards.title: Traditional vs Agentic Workflows
cards.body: Traditional automation is like giving step-by-step instructions to a robot. Agentic workflows are like hiring a smart assistant — you describe the outcome, they figure out how to get there.

analogy.text: It's like the difference between micromanaging every task versus hiring a competent employee and letting them solve problems independently.

bullets: Traditional: You configure every node and connection
Agentic: You describe the goal in plain English
Traditional: You debug every error manually
Agentic: The system fixes itself and learns

source.author: Nate Herk | AI Automation
source.description: The fundamental shift from traditional to agentic automation

cards.title: Quick Check
cards.question: What's the main difference between traditional and agentic workflows?

options.text: Agentic workflows are faster to execute
You describe outcomes, not steps, and the agent figures out how to achieve them
Agentic workflows don't need any setup

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! You focus on what you want, not how to get there.
cards.wrongFeedback: Not quite. The key difference is that you describe the desired outcome and let the agent determine the steps.

cards.title: Self-Healing: The Game Changer
cards.body: When your workflow breaks, instead of you reading error messages and fixing code, the agent reads the error, tests a fix, and updates its own code. It only calls you in if it's really stuck.
bullets: Updates its own code and instructions
Learns to avoid the same mistake
cards.safe: You don't need to read or write any code — the agent handles the technical details while you approve changes.

source.author: Nate Herk | AI Automation
source.description: How self-healing workflows automatically fix errors and improve over time

cards.title: Install Claude Code
cards.body: Let's get Claude Code set up in VS Code so you can start building agentic workflows.
bullets: Sign in with your Claude account
helpItems: Need a Claude account? Sign up at claude.ai
Make sure you have VS Code version 1.70 or higher

try.prompt: Practice the installation steps and verify Claude Code is working

commands.input: Open VS Code and check Extensions

commands.output: Claude Code extension appears in results
commands.hint: Look for the official Anthropic extension

cards.title: Natural Language Control (For Real)
cards.body: Unlike tools that just convert prompts to code, new agentic systems interview you first. They ask clarifying questions before writing any code, getting much closer to production-ready results.
bullets: What happens if X goes wrong?
cards.concept: The agent asks questions you wouldn't think of until problems arise weeks later. This front-loaded discovery prevents most issues before they happen.

source.author: Nate Herk | AI Automation
source.description: How modern agentic tools interview you to understand requirements fully

cards.title: Start Your First Agentic Project
cards.body: Create a new project and describe your automation goal in plain English. Don't write any code — let the agent figure out the technical implementation.
bullets: Open Claude Code in VS Code
Start by describing your automation goal in plain English
Example: 'I want to automatically summarize meeting transcripts from Fireflies and create tasks in ClickUp based on action items'
cards.code:
// Don't write this - just describe in natural language:
'Take Fireflies transcripts, extract action items, create ClickUp tasks'
cards.codeCaption: Describe the outcome, not the implementation steps
cards.warn: Resist the urge to specify technical details. Let the agent ask you what it needs to know.

cards.title: Halfway There!
cards.body: You've grasped the core concepts of agentic workflows and started your first project. Next up: we'll handle the agent's questions and set up security guardrails.

cards.title: Answer the Agent's Questions Fully
cards.body: When the agent asks clarifying questions, answer them thoroughly. These questions identify gaps that would cause problems later in production.
bullets: Edge cases: What if the API is down?
User permissions: Who can trigger this?
Data limits: How much data are we processing?
Error handling: What should happen if something fails?
cards.safe: The agent's questions often reveal issues you hadn't considered. Taking time here prevents debugging later.

cards.title: Set Security Guardrails
cards.body: Use natural language to define what should never happen in your workflow. The agent will enforce these constraints automatically.
cards.code:
Never send customer emails to third-party tools
Stop if API costs exceed $5
Always encrypt sensitive data
Don't log personal information
cards.codeCaption: State your security requirements in plain English
cards.warn: Be specific about sensitive data handling — the agent will follow your exact instructions.

cards.title: Security Check
cards.question: How do agentic workflows handle security?

options.text: You set guardrails in natural language and the agent enforces them
Security is handled by the platform provider
You need to write security code manually

cards.correctFeedback: Right! You define security rules in plain English, and the agent continuously enforces them.
cards.wrongFeedback: The agent enforces security rules that you define in natural language.

cards.title: Parallel Agent Testing
cards.body: Instead of guessing which approach is best, you can run multiple agents simultaneously to test different solutions and compare results.
bullets: Run each approach with a separate agent
Pick the winner based on real results

analogy.text: Like having 5 developers build the same feature in parallel, then choosing the best implementation — except it happens in minutes, not weeks.

source.author: Nate Herk | AI Automation
source.description: Running multiple agents in parallel to test different approaches

cards.title: Gather Your API Keys
cards.body: Collect API keys for all the tools you want to connect. This is your only technical task — the agent handles all the integration complexity.
bullets: Fireflies: Get API key from settings
ClickUp: Generate key in Apps section
Slack: Create app and get token
helpItems: Most APIs have free tiers for testing
Save keys in a secure password manager
Check rate limits for each service
cards.warn: Never commit API keys to version control or share them in screenshots.

cards.title: Let the Agent Build
cards.body: Provide your API keys to the agent and let it handle all the technical integration work. You just monitor progress and approve changes.
bullets: Give the agent your API keys
cards.safe: The agent will show you what it's building and ask for approval before making changes. You stay in control.

try.prompt: Practice handing off to the agent and monitoring its work

commands.hint: The agent will ask for keys as needed

commands.output: Agent shows workflow structure and asks for approval
commands.hint: You can ask for changes before approving

cards.title: Natural Language Updates
cards.body: Once your workflow is running, you can modify it using plain English commands. No coding required — just tell it what you want changed.
bullets: 'Make it run every hour instead of daily'
cards.concept: Natural language becomes your remote control for the entire automation system.

cards.title: Final Check
cards.question: What's the biggest advantage of agentic workflows?

options.text: They run faster than traditional workflows
They fix themselves when they break and adapt to changes

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Self-healing eliminates the debugging loop that consumes most automation maintenance time.
cards.wrongFeedback: The killer feature is self-healing — agents that fix their own problems and learn from mistakes.

cards.title: You're Ready for Agentic Automation!
cards.body: You've learned to build self-healing workflows that adapt and improve automatically. You can now describe outcomes in plain English and let AI agents handle the technical complexity. Welcome to the future of automation!
